incorrects.description: Student clicked a wrong category on first attempt

steps.text: Let's look closely—count the symbols in each column.

incorrects.description: Student clicked a wrong category on second attempt

steps.text: Let's think about this together. James has 6 symbols. Remember, each symbol stands for 2 books, so that's 12 books total.

incorrects.description: Student clicked a wrong category three or more times — system models the answer

params.description: System counts each column: Maya 4 symbols = 8 books, James 6 symbols = 12 books, Sofia 3 symbols = 6 books, Aiden 5 symbols = 10 books. James column highlights as the tallest.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. First, I count each column. Maya has 4 symbols—that's 8 books. James has 6 symbols—that's 12 books. Sofia has 3 symbols—that's 6 books. Aiden has 5 symbols—that's 10 books. James has the most symbols, so James read the most books.
